_note: Delegation budget contract (delegation_budget_contract_v1): one file per delegated computation / verification workstream, written by the coordinator BEFORE dispatch, kept under team/delegations/. A delegated executor's default drift is to refine precision indefinitely and to expand scope; a delegation without explicit budgets is drift by construction. Validation is fail-closed (check_delegation_budget.py): a missing field, an unfilled <placeholder>, or an unknown contract_version does not pass.
workstream: <one line: what is delegated (the computation / verification task, its deliverable)>

tolerance_ceiling._note: Numeric tolerance ceiling the result must reach and must NOT be refined beyond: reaching the ceiling means STOP, not 'keep improving'. The anchor note states which requirement of the task derives the ceiling (what the result is for — e.g. the precision at which the downstream decision changes), never what the method can achieve.
tolerance_ceiling.anchor_note: <one line: which task requirement derives this ceiling>

time_box._note: Hard wall-clock budget for the whole workstream. On expiry the executor wraps up from the atomic results already flushed to disk (see the research-harness long-running-jobs contract) — it never voids the batch.

max_attempts: <positive integer: cap on 'one last attempt' retries; exhausting it means wrap up, not retry>
scope_negative_list: <expansions the executor must NOT undertake on its own initiative, e.g.:>
<no building a full test suite beyond the delegated checks>

peak_memory_estimate._note: Measured on a single-unit dry run BEFORE the full launch; estimating wall-clock alone is not a resource estimate. heap_limit_mb is the explicit cap the full run is launched with and must be >= dry_run_peak_rss_mb.
peak_memory_estimate.dry_run_peak_rss_mb: <finite positive number: peak RSS of the single-unit dry run, in MB>
peak_memory_estimate.heap_limit_mb: <finite positive number: explicit heap cap for the full run, in MB>
peak_memory_estimate.dry_run_ref: <optional: pointer to the dry-run log / checkpoint that produced the measurement>

on_budget_exhausted: wrap up from flushed atomic results; record abandoned approaches in the failed-approaches ledger (failed_approaches_v1)
